question: Will CPI rise more than 1.3% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has been released and shows a monthly increase of 0.3% (seasonally adjusted), which is significantly below the 1.3% threshold required for YES resolution. Multiple sources confirm this actual result, making the probability of YES essentially 0%. The market appears to be pricing in some residual uncertainty despite the clear data.
key_factors: March 2026 CPI actual monthly increase was 0.3% (seasonally adjusted)
BLS official data confirms the reading is well below the 1.3% threshold
Multiple financial news sources corroborate the 0.3% figure
The report was released on April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET as scheduled
risks: Potential data revisions (though historically rare and small)
Market may still price in minimal probability due to any uncertainty about final calculations
Seasonal adjustment methodology changes (extremely unlikely to affect magnitude needed)
